📑💵 **UAE may switch oil sales to yuan without US lifeline — Report**

As the Iran war cuts off Hormuz oil revenue, the UAE has entered talks with the US for a dollar currency swap line, the WSJ reported.

Emirati officials warned: without it, a severe dollar shortage could force them to sell oil in yuan instead.

According to the report, the request faces hurdles at the Fed, which typically restricts such lines.

👍 **US-Israel-Iran war | ****@geopolitics_prime**
headline: 📑💵 **UAE may switch oil sales to yuan without US lifeline — Report**

👹🗣 **Netanyahu's chainsaw-wielding fanboy arrives in Israel to get Teacher's Gold Star**

🇦🇷 Argentina’s president arrived in Israel Sunday to launch the Isaac Accords, a policy package designed to strengthen Tel Aviv’s links to Latin America (classifying Israel’s enemies as ‘terrorists’, recognizing Jerusalem as the capital, ‘combating antisemitism’ by adopting the IHRA’s definition of antisemitism meaning being anti-Israel).

💬 “It warms my heart that you’re here,” Netanyahu told Milei, joking about how “Javier” is similar to “chaver” – the Hebrew word for friend.

♦️ Milei could not contain his excitement, following Netanyahu around like a schoolboy, giggling and calling attention to their matching blue and white tie and shirt combo.

♦️ At a press conference, Bibi told Milei he was “now part of the mishpacha – family, in the deepest sense of the word.”  

♦️ Milei will also get a Presidential Medal of Honor from Isaac Herzog, light a torch at Israel’s Independence Day festivities, and get an honorary PhD from Bar-Ilhan University.

♦️ Amid [Israel’s ongoing attacks on Christian shrines](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/68458) in Lebanon, the only thing missing was Bibi giving the leader of the predominantly Catholic Latin American country a little baggie with 20 pieces of silver.

full_text:
🇺🇸🛥 **Here’s why US Navy’s massive nuclear subs will be sitting ducks if they show up in the Persian Gulf**

🔴 America’s 6.9k ton Los Angeles-class fast attack subs and 18.7k ton nuclear-capable Ohio-class boomer cousins are simply too clunky to operate in the Gulf, with the waterway’s average depth of just 160 feet (49M) too shallow to accommodate the gigantic vessels, which measure 110M and 170M long, respectively.

🔴 AiTelly points out that while it’s too shallow for US subs to operate, the Gulf is [“perfect for Iran’s fleet of diesel-electric minisubs,” ](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/67663)including the Ghadir class, which “can sit silently on the seafloor waiting for a target to pass overhead before firing torpedoes.”

🔴 Then there’s the Gulf’s high noise levels thanks to commercial traffic, a rocky, uneven seabed and its waters’ warm, salty composition and high evaporation rates, all of which messes with sonar and weakens its ability to detect the acoustic signatures of subs (especially smaller ones like Ghadirs, which measure just 29M long).

🔴 Finally, there’s [Iran’s naval armaments](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/65262) to account for, including standard 533 mm torpedoes like supercavitating Hoots, heavy Valfajrs and long-range Jask-2s fired by subs, plus smaller 324 mm torpedoes carried by[ Iran’s vast mosquito fleet.](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/68409)

🔴 Bottom line? Iran can close Hormuz using a single, 7 crewmember Ghadir-class sub, and there’s not a thing Trump can do about it. Hence his renewed impotent rage-induced threats to attack “every single power plant and bridge” in the country.

👍 **US-Israel-Iran war | ****@geopolitics_prime**
headline: 🇺🇸🛥 **Here’s why US Navy’s massive nuclear subs will be sitting ducks if they show up in the Persian Gulf**

full_text:
🇮🇱 **Israel turning southern Lebanon into Gaza-like ‘yellow line’ **

Just days after a fragile ceasefire was agreed, the Israeli military announced the creation of a so-called “yellow line” in Lebanon—exactly like the deadly demarcation the IDF uses in Gaza.

__What’s it all about? __

🔴  This yellow line establishes a de facto military buffer zone stretching north of the border inside southern Lebanon, where Israeli forces can shoot at anyone who dares to approach from the Lebanese side 

🔴   Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu dubbed the line “a security strip 10 kilometers deep, which is much stronger and more intense than what we had previously” 

🔴  Lebanon and Hezbollah have rejected the creation of the line as an occupation of sovereign territory, which they say violates the very premise of the 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon that has been in effect since April 17

🔴   Israeli military has already attacked what it described as “terrorists,” who were allegedly trying to approach the IDF from the yellow line, which is already referred to as the “continuation of the Gazafication of southern Lebanon”

In Gaza, Israeli troops routinely fire on anyone approaching the yellow line, which has been in place since the October 10 ceasefire came into force between the IDF and Hamas. Israeli attacks have since then killed more than 700 people and wounded around 2,000 in Gaza.

👍 **US-Israel-Iran war | ****@geopolitics_prime**
headline: 🇮🇱 **Israel turning southern Lebanon into Gaza-like ‘yellow line’ **
